metadata.purpose: Cultural knowledge atoms for fil-PH, lo-LA, or-IN

culture_refs.expression: Maintaining smooth interpersonal relationships and group harmony is paramount
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Position products as enhancing group belonging and social harmony

culture_refs.expression: Deep concern for social acceptance and avoiding embarrassment or losing face
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid direct confrontation; use indirect, respectful messaging

culture_refs.expression: Strong sense of reciprocity and obligation to return favors
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Frame loyalty programs as mutual gratitude; emphasize lasting relationships

culture_refs.expression: Community helping each other, especially in times of need
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Highlight community benefits and collective success stories

culture_refs.expression: Family is the center of Filipino life; decisions consider family impact
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Show multi-generational benefits; family testimonials resonate

culture_refs.expression: Use of honorifics and deference to older or senior individuals
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use respectful language; show deference in formal communications

culture_refs.expression: Welcoming guests warmly is a core cultural value
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Position brand as welcoming and inclusive

taboos.reason: Causes loss of face (hiya) and damages relationships permanently
taboos.alternative: Use indirect communication, private discussions, or intermediaries

taboos.reason: Declining food or gifts is seen as rejecting the host's generosity
taboos.alternative: Accept graciously, even a small portion

taboos.reason: Filipinos avoid raising voices and showing anger publicly
taboos.alternative: Maintain calm, polite demeanor in all interactions

taboos.reason: Relationships take time to build; hasty decisions are seen as disrespectful
taboos.alternative: Allow time for relationship building and consensus

taboos.reason: Age hierarchy is fundamental; ignoring it is deeply offensive
taboos.alternative: Always greet elders first; use honorific titles

audience_traits.description: Philippines has one of highest social media usage rates globally
Overseas Filipino Workers shape consumption patterns and aspirations
High trust in celebrity and influencer recommendations

patterns.template: Sumali na sa {product} at maranasan ang pagkakaiba!

patterns.translation: Trusted by millions of Filipino families

culture_refs.expression: Buddhism permeates daily life; monks and temples hold central reverence
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Respect Buddhist values; avoid frivolous or materialistic messaging

culture_refs.expression: Preserving face and avoiding conflict is paramount in all interactions
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use soft, harmonious imagery and indirect communication

culture_refs.expression: The universal greeting reflects cultural warmth and well-wishing
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Incorporate Sabaidee in campaigns for cultural authenticity

culture_refs.expression: Age and social status determine appropriate behavior and language
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use formal titles (Than, Nai, Nang) appropriately

culture_refs.expression: Physical and behavioral modesty is expected in public
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Avoid flashy or ostentatious imagery; promote simplicity

culture_refs.expression: Supporting local businesses and community reflects goodwill
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Highlight local partnerships and community benefits

taboos.reason: The head is considered sacred and the highest part of the body
taboos.alternative: Never touch anyone's head, even children

taboos.reason: Feet are considered the lowest and dirtiest part of the body
taboos.alternative: Never point feet at people, Buddha images, or temples

taboos.reason: Physical intimacy in public is considered inappropriate
taboos.alternative: Maintain modest, respectful distance in public

taboos.reason: Causes loss of face and disrupts harmony
taboos.alternative: Speak softly and calmly; resolve issues privately

taboos.reason: Monks are highly revered; women must not touch them
taboos.alternative: Show utmost respect; women maintain physical distance

taboos.reason: Patience is valued; haste can seem disrespectful
taboos.alternative: Allow time for consensus and relationship building

audience_traits.description: Increasing smartphone adoption, especially among youth
Strong adherence to Buddhist and traditional practices
Social standing and reputation highly important

expressions.text: ຂອບໃຈຈ້າວ (Khop jai jao)

patterns.template: ເຂົ້າຮ່ວມ {product} ມື້ນີ້
ສະບາຍດີ! ຍິນດີຕ້ອນຮັບ

culture_refs.expression: Lord Jagannath holds central significance in Odia life and culture
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Respect Jagannath traditions; avoid commercial exploitation of sacred imagery

culture_refs.expression: Odisha's temples are central to cultural identity and daily practice
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Reference temple heritage respectfully; emphasize spiritual connections

culture_refs.expression: Odissi dance originated in temples; represents regional pride
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Celebrate classical arts in culturally appropriate ways

culture_refs.expression: Respect and politeness are fundamental to Odia interactions
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Use respectful, courteous language; show patience

culture_refs.expression: Active efforts to preserve indigenous heritage against Western influence
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Celebrate traditional practices; avoid undermining local culture

culture_refs.expression: Major festivals like Rath Yatra, Nuakhai, Pousa Purnima are central
culture_refs.marketing_angle: Align campaigns with festival calendars; respect traditions

taboos.reason: Jagannath is the heart of Odia identity; any disrespect is deeply offensive
taboos.alternative: Show utmost reverence; avoid commercial use of sacred imagery

taboos.reason: Sacred spaces require permission and respect
taboos.alternative: Seek appropriate permissions; be mindful of sacred areas

taboos.reason: Sensitivity about preserving indigenous culture from external influences
taboos.alternative: Adapt to local culture; don't impose foreign values

taboos.reason: Politeness markers are expected in commercial and social interactions

audience_traits.description: Strong identification with Odia heritage and Odisha state
Consumer behavior strongly tied to festival calendar
Daily life intertwined with religious practices

expressions.context: Victory to Lord Jagannath - common blessing
